title: FDA Certification Online in India – Registration, Cost & Help
metaDescription: Get FDA certification in India with online registration support, document preparation, cost clarity, and expert consultant help for fast approval & renewal compliance.

hero.headline: FDA Certification in India – Online Registration, Cost & Renewal
hero.subtext: Navigate FDA regulations, avoid import bans, and expand confidently with Professionals Law.

introduction.heading: Introduction and Its Compliance
body: FDA Certification refers to the approval or registration process governed by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), a federal agency of the United States. It is an important regulation for companies that manufacture, package, label or distribute foods, drugs, cosmetics, medical devices and dietary supplements for the U.S. market. With FDA certification, the products meet US safety, health, and labelling standards. Professionals Law helps contact-makers in India to obtain FDA registration and approvals thereby providing a full end to end service, which includes product classification, documentation assistance, establishment registration, agent appointment and compliance review. Professionals Law also ensures that your products are FDA compliant and prepared to enter one of the most highly regulated and profitable markets in the world.

whyNeeded.heading: Why It Is Needed
body: FDA Certification is a mandatory requirement for exporting food, drugs, and medical devices to the U.S. That helps bring confidence that your product is safe, accurately labelled and made according to standards. Without F.D.A. approval or registration, your goods may be detained or refused at ports of entry to the United States. Professionals Law contributes significantly in aiding Indian exporters adhere to the USFDA’s maze of regulation with all documentation ready, facility in conformity and suitable submission that saves Indian exporters from facing undue hold, penalty, rejection in international trade.

body.title: Market Access to the U.S.
body.description: FDA certification allows legal entry of your products into the United States, one of the largest consumer markets globally.

body.title: Enhanced Product Credibility
body.description: Certification from the FDA boosts trust among global consumers and business partners due to its strict safety and quality standards.

body.title: Competitive Advantage
body.description: FDA-compliant products are perceived as high-quality, giving you a competitive edge in both domestic and international markets.

body.title: Compliance with Global Standards
body.description: Meeting FDA norms ensures alignment with international safety regulations, making further market expansion easier.

body.title: Avoidance of Regulatory Risks
body.description: Proper FDA registration reduces the risk of shipment rejections, product recalls, or import alerts.

criteria:
Manufacturers, exporters, and distributors of FDA-regulated products such as:
Food and beverages
Cosmetics
Dietary supplements
Pharmaceuticals
Medical devices
Facility must be located outside the U.S. and must appoint a U.S. Agent
Product should meet labelling, formulation, and packaging requirements set by FDA guidelines

documents: U.S. Agent authorization letter (for foreign facilities)
FDA product code and intended use

body.description: Determine if the product falls under FDA-regulated categories.
Register the foreign manufacturing facility with FDA.
Designate a U.S.-based agent for communication with FDA.
Collect required product, facility, and label information.
Complete FDA registration or premarket approval process as applicable.
Receive registration confirmation or product approval.

body.title: Penalties
body.description:
Detention or seizure of shipments by U.S. Customs
Import Alerts or Red List entries
Monetary fines and sanctions
Reputational damage and potential blacklisting
Legal action for misbranding or adulteration

The timeline varies based on the product category:
Facility Registration: 7 to 15 days
Drug/Device Approval: 3 months to 2 years (if clinical trials or premarket approval is needed)

body.question: Is FDA Certification mandatory for exports to the U.S.?
body.answer: Yes, for all FDA-regulated products like food, drugs, cosmetics, and medical devices.

body.question: What is the difference between FDA registration and approval?
body.answer: Registration allows your facility to operate; approval is required for specific product categories like drugs or high-risk medical devices.

body.question: Does FDA inspect foreign facilities?
body.answer: Yes, FDA may inspect foreign manufacturing units to ensure compliance.

body.question: What is a U.S. Agent and why is it required?
body.answer: A U.S. Agent is a liaison between your business and the FDA and is mandatory for non-U.S. facilities.

body.question: How long is FDA registration valid?
body.answer: Most registrations are valid for one year and must be renewed annually.

body.question: Can Professionals Law help in appointing a U.S. Agent?
body.answer: Yes, Professionals Law assists in connecting with reliable U.S. Agents for compliance.

body.question: What happens if FDA compliance is violated?
body.answer: Violations can result in import bans, fines, and legal consequences.

body.question: Is FDA approval required for cosmetics?
body.answer: No pre-approval is required for cosmetics, but they must still comply with labelling and safety standards.

body.question: Can Professionals Law handle the entire FDA registration process?
body.answer: Yes, Professionals Law provides complete support—from classification to final registration and ongoing compliance.
